sections.title: What reported speech does (core logic)
sections.content:
Reported (**indirect**) speech repeats what someone said inside your own sentence — tense, pronouns and time words adjust to your viewpoint.
- Clause Linking root (#4): the report is an **embedded subordinate clause** — same engineering as relative clauses and conditionals; embedding triggers the **backshift** tense shift (Verb Phrase root #2 is secondary).
- Everywhere: your boss's words, news and stories, B2 sentence transformation, formal emails (*She mentioned that…*).

items.label: Structure
items.formula: reporting verb (said, told, asked) in the main clause + (that) + embedded clause — that is optional

diagrams.text: Czech does NOT backshift tenses the same way — direct translation gives *He said he is tired*, a common B1/B2 error. When the reporting verb goes past, the reported tense usually goes back one step too.

sections.title: The backshift rule (statements)
sections.content:
Reporting verb in the **past** (*said, told, asked*) → the tense inside the reported clause usually shifts **one step back in time**.
- *past simple → past perfect* applies in careful/formal use.
- The single highest-impact rule in the topic and the one Czech speakers most often skip — tested in every B1/B2 sentence-transformation exercise.

items.good: "I **have seen** it." → She said she **had seen** it.
"I **must go**." → She said she **had to** go.

sections.title: When NOT to backshift
sections.content:
Backshift is the default, but not automatic — some reports keep the original tense.
The **overshoot trap** for Czech speakers: once the rule is learned, the temptation is to backshift everything, even universal truths. Both extremes (no shift at all / shift everything) sound wrong.

steps.q: Is the reporting verb present (*says*, *tells*, *is saying*)?
Universal truth, or still true now?
Just said, and the situation hasn't changed?

items.good: She said the Earth **orbits** the sun.
He told me he **lives** in Prague.

items.why: reporting verb is present — no backshift

items.good: He just said he **wants** to come.
He said he **was** a doctor. / He said he **is** a doctor.
items.why: default backshift / keep present if still true and you want to emphasise that

sections.title: Said vs told (and other reporting verbs)
sections.content:
The two most common reporting verbs split on **who comes next**.
*He said me…* is a high-frequency Czech B1 error. Variety in reporting verbs is a B2 writing-and-speaking marker — using only *said* the whole time flattens your English.

left.title: say — no person object
points: or with **to**: *She said to me that…*

right.title: tell — person required
points: quick test: *tell me*, *tell him*, *tell us* — yes

items.good: He **told me** that he was tired.
items.bad: He told that he was tired.
He said me that he was tired.

sections.title: Reported questions
sections.content:
Reporting a question turns it back into a statement: no **question mark**, no **do/does/did**, and SUBJECT + VERB order.
Czech keeps question word order in indirect questions; English doesn't — *He asked where I lived* is a clear B1/B2 marker, tested constantly. The second classic trap after backshift.

items.label: Yes/No question
items.formula: asked + if/whether + subject + verb → "Are you busy?" → She asked if/whether I was busy.

items.label: Wh- question
items.formula: asked + wh-word + subject + verb → "Where do you live?" → He asked where I lived.

items.bad: She asked what time is it.
He asked where was I going.
She asked if could I swim.

sections.title: Reported commands and requests
sections.content:
Commands and requests use a completely different pattern from statements — no *that*-clause, no backshift.
- **tell** = instruction or order (*She told me to leave.*); **ask** = request, more polite (*She asked me to leave.*)
- Czech speakers often try *He said me to close the door* — wrong on two counts: *said me* and the said + to-infinitive pattern. Commands appear all the time in real reporting: instructions at work, parental rules, exam scenarios.

items.label: Command
items.formula: "Close the door." → She told me to close the door.

items.label: Request
items.formula: "Please wait." → He asked me to wait.

items.label: Negative
items.formula: "Don't worry." → She told him not to worry. (not before to)

items.bad: He said to me sit down.

items.good: She **told me not to be** late.

sections.title: Pronoun, time and place shifts
sections.content:
Backshift is only one layer — pronouns, time words and place words shift to match the new speaker, time and place too.
- Also: *you* → *I/me/he/she*, *tonight* → *that night*, *next week* → *the following week*; *yesterday/tomorrow* can also be *the previous day / the following day*.
- Forgetting these breaks the report: *He said he saw her yesterday* means yesterday from YOUR point of view today — if he said it last week, you need *the day before*. High-yield in exam transformations.

items.good: "**I** lost **my** keys." → She said **she** had lost **her** keys.
"**I** met **your** brother **here yesterday**." → She said **she** had met **my** brother **there the day before**.

sections.title: The six Czech-speaker traps
sections.content:
These six errors cover roughly 90% of mistakes Czech B1/B2 learners make in reported speech.
Spotting and fixing them gives a visible jump in accuracy.

items.bad: He asked where do I live.
items.why: question word order in reported questions

items.good: She **told me** that she would come. / She **said** that she would come.
items.bad: She told that she would come.

items.good: He **told me** he was busy. / He **said to me** he was busy.
items.bad: He said me he was busy.

items.good: He **told me to close** the door.
items.bad: He said me to close the door.

sections.title: Practice roadmap & next steps
sections.content:
Start here after the cards:
- Guided MC (backshift, *say/tell*, question order)
- Gap fill (transformations from direct to reported)
- Error correction (fix the Czech-speaker trap sentences)

For the underlying tense forms (past simple, past perfect, past continuous), see [[topic:tenses_general|Tenses: General]]. Reported speech sits next to [[topic:conditionals|Conditionals]] and [[topic:relative_pronouns|Relative Pronouns]] in the Clause Linking root — same embedding logic, different shape.

questions.question: "I am leaving tomorrow." Which reported sentence is correct?

options.a: She said she was leaving the next day.
options.b: She said she is leaving tomorrow.
options.c: She said she was leaving tomorrow.
options.d: She said she had left the next day.

questions.explanation: Backshift: am leaving → was leaving; tomorrow → the next day.

questions.question: "Where do you live?" → He asked …
questions.explanation: Reported question: statement order (where I lived) and past backshift.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?

options.a: She told me that she would come.
options.b: She told that she would come.
options.c: She said me that she would come.
options.d: She told to me that she would come.

questions.explanation: Tell + person (tell me). Say does not take indirect object without 'to'.

questions.question: "Close the window." → She …

options.a: said me to close the window
options.c: told me to close the window
options.d: told to me close the window

questions.explanation: Reported command: tell + person + to-infinitive.

questions.question: "I have finished my work." → He said …
questions.explanation: Present perfect → past perfect in reported speech: had finished.

questions.question: "Are you coming?" → She asked me …
questions.explanation: Yes/No reported question: if + statement order (if I was coming).

questions.question: "I am tired." → She said she _____ tired.
questions.explanation: Backshift: present (am) → past (was) in reported speech.

questions.question: "We will help." → They said they _____ help.
questions.explanation: Will → would in reported speech.

questions.question: "Where do you live?" → He asked me where I _____.
questions.explanation: Reported question: statement order; present → past (lived).

questions.question: "I have seen the film." → She said she _____ seen the film.
questions.explanation: Present perfect → past perfect (had seen).

questions.question: "Close the door." → He _____ me to close the door.
questions.explanation: Reported command: tell + person + to-infinitive.

questions.question: "I can swim." → He said he _____ swim.
questions.explanation: Can → could in reported speech.

questions.question: "What is your name?" → She asked what my name _____.
questions.explanation: Reported question: what my name was (statement order).

questions.question: "I saw her yesterday." → He said he had seen her _____.
questions.explanation: Yesterday → the day before in reported speech.

questions.question: "Are you ready?" → He asked me _____ I was ready.
questions.explanation: Yes/No reported questions use **if** or whether.

questions.question: "I must go now." → She said she _____ to go then.
questions.explanation: Must → had to; now → then.

questions.question: "I am tired." → She said she _____ tired.
questions.explanation: Present → past: am → was.

questions.question: "We will help." → They said they _____ help.
questions.explanation: Will → would in reported speech.

questions.question: "Where do you live?" → He asked me where I _____.
questions.explanation: Reported question: statement order; present → past.

questions.question: "I have seen the film." → She said she _____ seen the film.
questions.explanation: Present perfect → past perfect: had seen.

questions.question: "Close the door." → He _____ me to close the door.
questions.explanation: Reported command: tell + person + to-infinitive.

questions.question: "I can swim." → He said he _____ swim.
questions.explanation: Can → could in reported speech.

questions.question: "What is your name?" → She asked (me) what my name _____.
questions.explanation: Reported question: what my name was (statement order).

questions.question: "I saw her yesterday." → He said he had seen her _____.
questions.explanation: Yesterday → the day before in reported speech.

questions.question: "Are you ready?" → He asked me _____ I was ready.
questions.explanation: Yes/No reported: if (or whether) + statement order.

questions.question: "I must go now." → She said she _____ to go then.
questions.explanation: Must → had to; now → then.

questions.question: Wrong: 'She said she is tired.' (reporting past words) Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Backshift: is → was when reporting in the past.

questions.question: Wrong: 'He asked where do I live.' Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Reported question: statement order (where I lived).

questions.question: Wrong: 'She told that she would come.' Correct the sentence.
answers: She told me that she would come.
She said that she would come.
She told me that she would come
She said that she would come
questions.explanation: Tell needs a person: tell me. Or use said (that).

questions.question: Wrong: 'He said me to close the door.' Correct the sentence.
answers: He told me to close the door.
He told me to close the door
questions.explanation: Commands use tell + person + to-infinitive, not say.

questions.question: Wrong: 'She said she has finished.' (reporting past words) Correct the sentence.
questions.explanation: Present perfect → past perfect: had finished.

questions.question: Wrong: 'He asked if am I coming.' Correct the sentence.
answers: He asked if I was coming.
He asked if I was coming
questions.explanation: Yes/No reported: if + statement order (if I was coming).

questions.question: Wrong: 'She said to me that she will help.' Correct the sentence. We are reporting something that was said in the past, so change the tense of the verb in the that-clause. (You may keep 'said to me' or use 'told me'.)
answers: She said to me that she would help.
She told me that she would help.
She said to me that she would help
She told me that she would help
questions.explanation: Will → would when reporting in the past.

questions.question: Wrong: 'They asked what time is it.' Correct the sentence.
answers: They asked what time it was.
They asked what time it was
questions.explanation: Reported question: statement order (what time it was).
